title: 5 tools I use to automate everything

slides.headline: I spend 2 hours/week running my business
slides.visual_note: Clock showing 2 hours, robot hand doing work

slides.headline: 1. Claude Code (overnight builds)
slides.body: Write a spec. Go to sleep. Wake up to working code. I've shipped 3 apps this way. $20/month.

slides.headline: 2. Make.com (workflow automation)
slides.body: Connects everything. New lead in form? Auto-add to CRM, send Slack alert, trigger email sequence. Zero code.

slides.headline: 3. Remotion (video factory)
slides.body: Programmatic video generation. One template = 100 variations. Each TikTok rendered automatically. $0/month (self-hosted).

slides.headline: 4. Playwright (web automation)
slides.body: Browser automation for anything. Scrape competitors. Auto-post to socials. Fill forms. Test apps. Free and open source.

slides.headline: 5. Clay (lead enrichment)
slides.body: Find signal on any company. Job changes, funding, tech stack. Auto-enrich leads. Skip the manual research. $150/month.
slides.visual_note: Lead card being enriched with data

slides.headline: How they connect
slides.body: Playwright scrapes opportunities. Clay enriches them. Make.com routes to CRM. Claude builds features. Remotion creates content.

slides.headline: Total monthly cost
slides.body: Claude: $20. Make: $15. Remotion: $0. Playwright: $0. Clay: $150. Total: $185/month for a full automation stack.

slides.headline: Time saved
slides.body: Before automation: 40 hours/week. After: 8 hours/week. Same output. More time to build new things.

cta_slide.headline: I share new automation setups weekly
